extra.reasoning:
The property requires that setting dizziness=true (all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity. dizziness appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest), which sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity) and requires dizziness as a conjunct. So flipping dizziness can only enable this lowest-acuity, highest-priority rule, creating a downward jump.

Counterexample baseline: back_pain=T, severe_headache=T, abdominal_pain=T, leg_swelling=T, rash=F, dizziness=F.
- dizziness=FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire (needs dizziness). Firing rules: raise_back_pain(p2,urgent), raise_abdominal_pain(p2,urgent), raise_severe_headache(p3,emergent), raise_leg_swelling(p3,emergent). Highest priority wins -> priority 3 -> acuity = emergent (3).
- dizziness=TRUE (witness): now ALL conjuncts of interaction_drop hold, so it fires at priority 100, the highest priority, beating the priority-3 emergent rules. acuity := self_care (0).

acuity drops from emergent (3) to self_care (0) when dizziness is set true, with every other input unchanged. This directly violates monotonicity. The witness records the dizziness=true assignment that produces the lowered output.
